Bus Timings

Everything You Need to Know About Muwaileh Bus Station in Sharjah

November 30, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Muwaileh Bus Terminal, located in Sharjah, is a vital transportation hub that connects residents and visitors to key destinations across the UAE. Known for its well-organized structure and convenient location, Muwaileh Bus Station offers seamless travel options for those commuting within Sharjah or to nearby emirates such as Dubai and Ajman. Whether you are a daily commuter or an occasional traveler, Muwaileh Bus Terminal serves as a reliable transit point with frequent bus services and essential amenities.

Address

Muwaileh, Sharjah United Arab Emirates

View Google Maps Directions Here.

Located in Sharjah, Muwaileh Bus Station is a key transit hub served by Routes 3, 14X, 77, and E315, connecting passengers to destinations across the city.

Bus Routes and Timings

Muwaileh Bus Terminal is served by several important routes that provide connectivity within Sharjah and beyond. Key bus routes include services to Al Rolla, Etisalat Bus Station, and Al Saja’a Terminal. Below are the major routes and their expected arrival times:

Bus Route Direction Expected Arrival Time
3 Al Rolla Terminal Every 30 minutes
14X Al Rolla Terminal Every 20 minutes
77 Al Saja’a Terminal Every 25 minutes
E315 Etisalat Bus Station Every 30 minutes

These routes provide comprehensive coverage and make Muwaileh Bus Terminal a key transit point for passengers traveling to various parts of Sharjah and neighboring emirates.

Bus Route Details

Muwaileh Bus Station is served by the following routes:

  • Route 3: Connects to Al Rolla Terminal with buses every 30 minutes.

  • Route 14X: Travels to Al Rolla Terminal every 20 minutes and returns to Muwaileh (alighting only).
  • Route 77: Serves Al Saja’a Terminal every 25 minutes and returns to Muwaileh (alighting only).
  • Route E315: Travels to Etisalat Bus Station with frequent service and returns to Muwaileh (alighting only).
Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

These routes connect passengers to key areas in Sharjah and beyond, with regular intervals ensuring reliable transportation for both early and late travelers.

Nearby Attractions

Muwaileh Bus Terminal’s location offers convenient access to several local attractions and amenities in Sharjah, including:

  • University City A hub for students, offering educational institutions like the American University of Sharjah and University of Sharjah.

  • Sahara Centre A popular shopping destination located just a short ride away, perfect for shopping enthusiasts and families.
  • Sharjah National Park A great recreational spot for families and visitors looking to enjoy green spaces.
  • Sharjah Cricket Stadium A major sporting venue in the region, known for hosting international cricket matches.

With these attractions nearby, Muwaileh Bus Terminal is more than just a transit point—it’s a gateway to Sharjah’s vibrant community and local amenities.

Nearby Stops

  1. Sharjah, Madina Shopping Centre 2(370m): E315

  2. Sharjah, Madina Shopping Centre 1(376m): E315

  3. Sharjah, Maliha Road, Madina Supermarket(490m): E315

  4. Sharjah, Maliha Road, Manama Supermarket 2(787m): E315

  5. Sharjah, Hala Building 2(803m): E315

Muwaileh Bus Terminal in Sharjah is a central hub for public transportation, offering reliable services to various destinations across the UAE. Whether you’re heading to work, shopping, or exploring Sharjah’s cultural attractions, Muwaileh Bus Terminal is an essential stop on your journey.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: November 30, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
